_template_instructions.purpose: Define the USE perspective: intended uses, user intents, and their mapping to core functionalities
_template_instructions.when_to_fill: After item/module extraction, before system aggregation
_template_instructions.how_to_use: 1. Copy to Output/<system>/system/defined/intended-use.json, 2. Fill in values, 3. Remove _template_instructions

product_context.product_description: <FILL: one paragraph describing what this product does>
product_context.medical_device_class: <FILL: Class I, IIa, IIb, or III per MDR>
product_context.software_safety_class: <FILL: A, B, or C per IEC 62304>

intended_use_and_context.purpose: <FILL: The clinical/healthcare purpose this device serves>

intended_users.required_training: <FILL: What training is required to use this safely>

intended_use_and_context.intended_environment: <FILL: Where is this used? e.g., Primary care clinics, Home environment>
intended_use_and_context.intended_patient_population: <FILL: Who are the patients? e.g., Adults seeking primary care>
contraindications: <FILL: Conditions where this product should NOT be used>
<FILL: e.g., Emergency situations requiring immediate in-person care>
limitations: <FILL: e.g., Not intended for diagnostic decisions>

intended_uses.name: <FILL: Short name, e.g., Clinical Appointment Management>
intended_uses.description: <FILL: What clinical need does this address? e.g., Enable healthcare providers and patients to schedule, manage, and track clinical appointments>
intended_uses.clinical_benefit: <FILL: What patient benefit results? e.g., Timely access to care, reduced administrative burden>

intended_uses.name: <FILL: e.g., Care Coordination>
intended_uses.description: <FILL: e.g., Facilitate handover, referral, and escalation of patient care between practitioners>
intended_uses.clinical_benefit: <FILL: e.g., Continuity of care, appropriate escalation of urgent cases>

user_intents.name: <FILL: e.g., Book Appointment>
user_intents.description: <FILL: e.g., Patient or practitioner schedules a new appointment>
user_intents._notes: Link to core functionalities identified in module extraction

user_intents.name: <FILL: e.g., Receive Appointment Reminder>
user_intents.description: <FILL: e.g., Patient receives notification about upcoming appointment>

user_intents.name: <FILL: e.g., View Appointment Details>
user_intents.description: <FILL: e.g., User views appointment information including time, practitioner, and notes>

user_intents.name: <FILL: e.g., Handover Patient>
user_intents.description: <FILL: e.g., Practitioner transfers patient care to another practitioner>

user_intents.name: <FILL: e.g., Escalate Critical Case>
user_intents.description: <FILL: e.g., System or practitioner escalates urgent patient situation>
user_intents._notes: This is safety-critical - links to essential performance

core_functionality_mapping._instructions: Map core functionalities from module extraction to user intents above

mappings.rationale: <FILL: Why this functionality enables this intent>

unmapped_functionalities.core_functionality_id: <FILL: Any CF without a user intent>
unmapped_functionalities.reason: <FILL: Why no user intent? Internal infrastructure? Should be removed?>
